texto: His being there was stirring up a lot of memories she'd have been happier to leave as sentimental sediment. Not that they weren't good memories. That was the trouble.
explicacao: Citação de Diana Killian sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “His being there was stirring up a lot of memories she'd have been happier to leave as sentimental…” articula reflexao, letting-go, memories em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

frase_original: His being there was stirring up a lot of memories she'd have been happier to leave as sentimental sediment. Not that they weren't good memories. That was the trouble.

texto: But what? But it was just so much easier to deal with the old pain by ignoring it? Forgetting it? Andy's presence meant having to actively work at forgiving him, and that was hard. Forgetting was much easier than forgiving--forgiving was an on-going process that had to continue past the dramatic declarations of apology and absolution.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Diana Killian que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “But what? But it was just so much easier to deal with the old pain by ignoring it? Forgetting it?…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, forgiveness, forgiving, com foco em trabalho e reflexao.

Forgetting was much easier than forgiving--forgiving was an on-going process that had to continue past the dramatic declarations of apology and absolution.
